Package global.thalion.ttio.providers

Interface StorageProvider

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable
All Known Implementing Classes:
Hdf5Provider, MemoryProvider, SqliteProvider, ZarrProvider
public interface StorageProvider extends AutoCloseable
Storage backend entry point.

Implementations are discovered via ServiceLoader. Each implementation must also expose a no-argument public constructor so the loader can instantiate it; the useful work happens in open(String, Mode).

Implementations are required to support the capability floor listed in docs/format-spec.md — hierarchical groups, typed 1-D datasets, partial reads, chunked storage, compression, compound datasets with VL strings, scalar and array attributes. Unsupported capabilities raise UnsupportedOperationException at the call site.

API status: Stable (Provisional per M39 — may change before v1.0).

Cross-language equivalents: Objective-C TTIOStorageProvider, Python ttio.providers.base.StorageProvider.

    StorageProvider.Mode
    Open modes mirroring h5py semantics.

  • Method Details

    • providerName

      String providerName()
      Short identifier used for logging and registry lookup.

    • supportsUrl

      boolean supportsUrl(String pathOrUrl)
      Return true if this provider supports opening the given path or URL. Used by the registry to pick a provider by scheme when the caller hasn't named one explicitly.

    • open

      StorageProvider open(String pathOrUrl, StorageProvider.Mode mode)
      Open the backing store. The provider instance is returned so chaining is possible: new Hdf5Provider().open(...).

      Re-opening an already-open provider is an error.

    • rootGroup

      StorageGroup rootGroup()
      Root group ("/"). Must be called after open(java.lang.String, global.thalion.ttio.providers.StorageProvider.Mode).

    • isOpen

      boolean isOpen()

    • nativeHandle

      @Deprecated(since="0.8", forRemoval=true) default Object nativeHandle()
      Deprecated, for removal: This API element is subject to removal in a future version.
      Scheduled for removal at v1.0. M43-M45 eliminated every internal caller; external callers should migrate to the StorageGroup / StorageDataset protocol. See docs/api-stability-v0.8.md §6.
      Return the underlying native storage handle — an Hdf5File for Hdf5Provider, null for MemoryProvider.

      Escape hatch for byte-level code (signatures, encryption, native compression filters) that cannot be expressed through the protocol. Any caller that invokes this is pinned to a specific backend.

    • supportsChunking

      default boolean supportsChunking()
      true if the backend honors chunkSize in StorageGroup.createDataset(java.lang.String, global.thalion.ttio.Enums.Precision, long, int, global.thalion.ttio.Enums.Compression, int). Defaults to false — only Hdf5Provider returns true. Memory and SQLite accept chunkSize for interface compatibility but silently ignore it.

    • supportsCompression

      default boolean supportsCompression()
      true if the backend honors compression / compressionLevel. Defaults to false. Only Hdf5Provider returns true (zlib + LZ4).

    • beginTransaction

      default void beginTransaction()
      Start a write-batching transaction. Default no-op (HDF5, Memory). SQLiteProvider overrides this to issue BEGIN on the underlying JDBC connection.

      Callers that wrap bulk loads in beginTransaction() / commitTransaction() get the SQLite batch speedup without the ad-hoc per-provider convention the M39 providers used before.

    • commitTransaction

      default void commitTransaction()
      Commit and end an open transaction started by beginTransaction(). Default no-op.

    • rollbackTransaction

      default void rollbackTransaction()
      Roll back and end an open transaction started by beginTransaction(). Default no-op.

    • close

      void close()
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
